title: CFTC Chair Selig Warns Regulators Will End Up ‘Writing All the Rules’ for Crypto if Clarity Act Stalls
summary: CFTC Chair Michael Selig warned that regulators may end up writing all the rules for crypto if Congress fails to pass the Clarity Act, potentially leading to a patchwork of inconsistent regulations across different agencies.
body:
CFTC Chair Michael Selig has issued a stark warning that regulators may end up writing all the rules for the crypto industry if Congress fails to pass the Clarity Act. Speaking at a recent industry conference, Selig emphasized the need for clear, bipartisan legislation to provide a stable regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies.

The Clarity Act is a proposed bill aimed at providing regulatory clarity for the crypto industry by defining the roles of various regulatory bodies, including the CFTC and the SEC. Selig warned that without this legislation, regulators may step in to fill the void, potentially leading to a fragmented and inconsistent regulatory environment. He highlighted the importance of Congress acting swiftly to avoid regulatory uncertainty that could stifle innovation and investor confidence.

For everyday crypto users, this means that the future of crypto regulation could be significantly impacted by political inaction. If the Clarity Act fails, different regulatory bodies may impose their own rules, leading to confusion and potential legal risks for crypto investors and businesses. This could also affect the availability and usability of crypto products and services.

The next steps for crypto enthusiasts and industry stakeholders are to watch closely for any developments in the Clarity Act's progress through Congress. Staying informed about regulatory changes and engaging with policymakers can help ensure a more favorable outcome for the crypto industry.
twitterThread: CFTC Chair Selig warns: If Congress doesn't pass the Clarity Act, regulators will end up 'writing all the rules' for crypto. This could create a messy patchwork of regulations.
The Clarity Act aims to define roles for CFTC and SEC in crypto regulation. Without it, different agencies may impose their own rules, causing confusion and legal risks.

If you care about crypto's future, watch the Clarity Act's progress in Congress. Stay informed and engaged to avoid a fragmented regulatory landscape. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-09-cftc-chair-warns-of-regulatory-overreach-if-crypto-clarity-act-fails
